finding_results.notes: Roughened Gloria's shampoo/father critique across four lines. Line 118: trimmed to 'The shampoo was manipulative' (blunter, fewer words). Line 122: broke the polished mini-speech into shorter, rougher Gloria-units with a repetition-restart ('Your father did that.' pause, then 'Harold used to do that.') using his proper name per her lexical signature; replaced abstract 'feel the most' with concrete 'feel sorry for you'; replaced literary 'you placed it' with blunt 'you picked it on purpose'; replaced 'the most wounded' with generational 'the worst off'. Line 126: replaced abstract 'would have made him uncomfortable' with concrete physical pair 'He'd have hated it. He'd have left the room.' and changed 'Which means' to blunter 'That means'; replaced 'paused' with 'stopped' and adjusted narrator beat. Line 130: removed clinical 'I'm giving you my assessment' — kept 'I'm not complimenting you' and jumped straight to 'Did you make money?' All three judgments preserved (depreciation too long, shampoo manipulative like Harold, father material accurate). No line now reads as a self-contained review blurb.

summary: Single finding addressed: Gloria's phone-call critique roughened from structured review-like speech into shorter, blunter, more interruptive Gloria-language with generational phrasing, proper-name repetition, and concrete physical detail. Three judgments intact; no sanitization; heading contract preserved; no collateral changes outside the cited dialogue passage.
